---
title: "PyTorch stack (libtorch / ExecuTorch)"
format: html
---

LibTorch and optional ExecuTorch are fetched under [`external/`](../../external/). Version pins and URLs live in [`pytorch_stack_urls.cmake`](../../external/pytorch_stack_urls.cmake).

## Version pins (independent)

| Cache variable | Default | Role |
|----------------|---------|------|
| `NEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_VERSION` | `2.9.0` | Which libtorch release to download |
| `NEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_CUDA_TAG` | `cu126` | PyTorch CUDA folder when `ENABLE_NEURALYZER_CUDA` is ON |
| `NEURALYZER_EXECUTORCH_GIT_TAG` | `v1.0.1` | ExecuTorch git tag |

`NEURALYZER_CUDA_VERSION` (e.g. `12.6`) is set from the manifest when your `(libtorch version, CUDA tag)` pair is resolved — it is not a separate user-facing pin.

Override at configure time, for example:

```bash
cmake --preset my-clang-release -DNEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_VERSION=2.5.1 -DNEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_CUDA_TAG=cu121
```

## URLs

[`pytorch_stack_urls.cmake`](../../external/pytorch_stack_urls.cmake) uses plain `if(NEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_VERSION …)` / `if(NEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_CUDA_TAG …)` blocks with **full zip URLs** under each version. PyTorch naming differs by platform and era (e.g. Linux cxx11-abi for 2.5.1). Paste exact links from:

- [CPU](https://download.pytorch.org/libtorch/cpu/)
- [cu126](https://download.pytorch.org/libtorch/cu126/)
- [cu121](https://download.pytorch.org/libtorch/cu121/)

When you add a new libtorch version, add a new `elseif(VERSION_EQUAL …)` section. When you add CUDA support for that version, add a nested `if(CUDA_TAG STREQUAL …)` with Linux/Windows CUDA URLs and append matching entries to `NEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_VERIFY_VERSIONS` and `NEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_VERIFY_CUDA_TAGS`.

## Verify

```bash
cmake -P external/verify_libtorch_urls.cmake
cmake -DNEURALYZER_VERIFY_ALL_LIBTORCH_CONFIGS=ON -P external/verify_libtorch_urls.cmake
```

## Other options

- `NEURALYZER_LIBTORCH_URL` — override the single URL used for this configure.
- `NEURALYZER_VERIFY_LIBTORCH_URL_AT_CONFIGURE` — probe the selected URL before FetchContent (needs `curl`).

CTest: `verify_libtorch_download_urls` (labels `network`, `external`).
